title: celtis pubescens var. chichape
intro: Celtis pubescens var. chichape, also known as Chichape, is a variety of the deciduous tree Celtis pubescens, native to South America. The bark of this plant is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Chichape include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, Chichape is prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the bark in water, or as a tincture by steeping the bark in a solvent such as ethanol.
